servinfo.title: Fail-Safe Table
servinfo.ptxt: When any of the following DTCs is detected, ESP® system is placed in fail-safe mode and ABS, EBD, traction control and stability control functions are deactivated until the fail-safe condition is resolved.

item.ptxt: *2: Fail-safe mode may or may not engage depending on ESP® system malfunction. Fail-safe mode can be determined by lighting status of ABS warning light, EBD warning light (brake warning light) or ESP® warning light.
When ABS warning light lights up, ABS control is deactivated.
When EBD warning light (brake warning light) lights up, EBD control is deactivated.
When ESP® warning light lights up, traction control and stability control are deactivated.

entry.ptxt: When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal when ESP® control module detects system as normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When solenoid valve is sufficiently cooled down. (After continuous operation, inlet solenoid valve requires 300 seconds of suspension and outlet solenoid valve requires 50 seconds of suspension.)
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal and “Driving Test” under ESP® System Check is performed after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When new steering angle sensor is installed.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
When ESP® control module judges the system to be normal after switching ignition from “OFF” to “ON”.
